channel.title: Your Name — Essays
channel.description: Essays on thinking, reading, work, and craft.

item.title: Why Write?
item.description: You do not write to record what you think. You write to find out what you think.

item.title: How to Read
item.description: Read what you love until you love to read. Then read the old books, slowly, twice.

item.title: Compounding
item.description: The most powerful force in a human life is getting one percent better at something you can sustain.

item.title: Taste
item.description: Taste is not a gift. It is a debt you accrue by caring more than is reasonable, and it is paid off in work.

item.title: The Busyness Trap
item.description: Busy is what you are when you have surrendered your schedule to everyone but yourself.

item.title: Notes on Speed
item.description: Fast is not frantic. The quickest people I know are serene — they just never wait for permission to begin.
